channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Microsoft links Medusa ransomware affiliate to zero-day attacks
item.description: Microsoft says that Storm-1175, a China-based financially motivated cybercriminal group known for deploying Medusa ransomware payloads, has been deploying n-day and zero-day exploits in high-velocity attacks. [...]

item.title: DPRK-Linked Hackers Use GitHub as C2 in Multi-Stage Attacks Targeting South Korea
item.description: Threat actors likely associated with the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) have been observed using GitHub as command-and-control (C2) infrastructure in multi-stage attacks targeting organizations in South...

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch exploited Fortinet EMS flaw by Friday
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) ordered federal agencies to secure FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) instances against an actively exploited vulnerability by Friday. [...]

item.title: Automated Credential Harvesting Campaign Exploits React2Shell Flaw
item.description: An emerging threat cluster tracked as UAT-10608 is exploiting vulnerable Web-exposed Next.js apps and using an automated tool to exfiltrate credentials, secrets, and other system data.

item.title: How LiteLLM Turned Developer Machines Into Credential Vaults for Attackers
item.description: The most active piece of enterprise infrastructure in the company is the developer workstation. That laptop is where credentials are created, tested, cached, copied, and reused across services, bots, build tools, and...

item.title: Guardarian Users Targeted With Malicious Strapi NPM Packages
item.description: Hackers published 36 NPM packages posing as Strapi plugins to execute shells, escape containers, and harvest credentials. The post Guardarian Users Targeted With Malicious Strapi NPM Packages appeared first on Securit...

item.title: North Korean Hackers Target High-Profile Node.js Maintainers
item.description: The threat actor behind the Axios supply chain attack has been aiming at other maintainers in its social engineering campaign. The post North Korean Hackers Target High-Profile Node.js Maintainers appeared first on Se...

item.title: Google Wants to Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography by 2029
item.description: Google says that it will fully transition to post-quantum cryptography by 2029. I think this is a good move, not because I think we will have a useful quantum computer anywhere near that year, but because crypto-agili...

item.title: Qilin and Warlock Ransomware Use Vulnerable Drivers to Disable 300+ EDR Tools
item.description: Threat actors associated with Qilin and Warlock ransomware operations have been observed using the bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) technique to silence security tools running on compromised hosts, according t...

item.title: BKA Identifies REvil Leaders Behind 130 German Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Germany's Federal Criminal Police Office (aka BKA or the Bundeskriminalamt) has unmasked the real identities of two of the key figures associated with the now-defunct REvil (aka Sodinokibi) ransomware-as-a-service (Ra...

item.title: Residential proxies make a mockery of IP-based defenses
item.description: Attack traffic moved through ordinary home and mobile connections in ways that limited the usefulness of IP reputation on its own. GreyNoise observed 4 billion malicious sessions during a 90-day period and described a...

item.title: CVE-2026-35616: Fortinet fixes actively exploited high-severity flaw
item.description: Fortinet issued emergency patches for a critical FortiClient EMS flaw (CVE-2026-35616) actively exploited in the wild. Fortinet released out-of-band patches for a critical FortiClient EMS vulnerability, tracked as CVE...

item.title: 36 Malicious npm Packages Exploited Redis, PostgreSQL to Deploy Persistent Implants
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 36 malicious packages in the npm registry that are disguised as Strapi CMS plugins but come with different payloads to facilitate Redis and PostgreSQL exploitation, deploy rev...

item.title: Fortinet Patches Actively Exploited CVE-2026-35616 in FortiClient EMS
item.description: Fortinet has released out-of-band patches for a critical security flaw impacting FortiClient EMS that it said has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-35616 (CVSS score: 9.1), has been de...

item.title: Introducing the Landing Zone Accelerator on AWS Universal Configuration and LZA Compliance Workbook
item.description: November 20, 2025: Date this information was first published. We’re pleased to announce the availability of the latest sample security baseline from Landing Zone Accelerator on AWS (LZA)—the Universal Configuration. D...

item.title: Qilin ransomware group claims the hack of German political party Die Linke
item.description: Qilin ransomware claims it stole data from Germany’s Die Linke and threatens to leak it; the party confirmed the incident, but not a breach. The Qilin ransomware group claims it stole data from Die Linke, a German pol...

item.title: U.S. CISA adds a flaw in TrueConf Client to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds a flaw in TrueConf Client to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added a f...

item.title: European Commission Confirms Data Breach Linked to Trivy Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Hackers stole over 300GB of data from the Commission’s AWS environment, including personal information. The post European Commission Confirms Data Breach Linked to Trivy Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityW...

item.title: Six Accounts, One Actor: Inside the prt-scan Supply Chain Campaign
item.description: After hackerbot-claw, another AI-powered campaign exploiting pull_request_target confirms the threat is here to stay. We trace the attacker back to three weeks before anyone noticed.

item.title: When an Attacker Meets a Group of Agents: Navigating Amazon Bedrock's Multi-Agent Applications
item.description: Unit 42 research on multi-agent AI systems on Amazon Bedrock reveals new attack surfaces and prompt injection risks. Learn how to secure your AI applications. The post When an Attacker Meets a Group of Agents: Navigat...

item.title: Hims & Hers warns of data breach after Zendesk support ticket breach
item.description: Telehealth giant Hims & Hers Health is warning that it suffered a data breach after support tickets were stolen from a third-party customer service platform. [...]

item.title: China-Linked TA416 Targets European Governments with PlugX and OAuth-Based Phishing
item.description: A China-aligned threat actor has set its sights on European government and diplomatic organizations since mid-2025, following a two-year period of minimal targeting in the region. The campaign has been attributed to T...

item.title: Do not get high(jacked) off your own supply (chain)
item.description: In the span of just a few weeks, we have observed a dizzying array of major supply chain attacks. If we are all building on such shaky foundation, what can we do to keep safe?

item.title: Apple Breaks Precedent, Patches DarkSword for iOS 18
item.description: Even organizations with users unwilling or unable to adopt iOS 26 can now protect themselves from a severe mobile OS-cracking tool.

item.title: Axios NPM supply chain incident
item.description: Overview of the recent Axios NPM supply chain incident including details of the payloads delivered from actor-controlled infrastructure.

item.title: Die Linke German political party confirms data stolen by Qilin ransomware
item.description: The Qilin ransomware group has claimed responsibility for an attack against Die Linke ('The Left'), forcing an IT systems outage at the political party, and threatening sensitive data leak. [...]

item.title: How AWS KMS and AWS Encryption SDK overcome symmetric encryption bounds
item.description: If you run high-scale applications that encrypt large volumes of data, you might be concerned about tracking encryption limits and rotating keys. This post explains how AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) and the AWS...

item.title: Microsoft Details Cookie-Controlled PHP Web Shells Persisting via Cron on Linux Servers
item.description: Threat actors are increasingly using HTTP cookies as a control channel for PHP-based web shells on Linux servers and to achieve remote code execution, according to findings from the Microsoft Defender Security Researc...

item.title: Blast Radius of TeamPCP Attacks Expands Amid Hacker Infighting
item.description: As organizations disclose breaches tied to TeamPCP's supply chain attacks, ShinyHunters and Lapsus$ are getting involved, taking credit, and creating a murky situation for enterprises.

item.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Asian Government Attacks
item.description: A Chinese threat actor exploited the video conferencing platform to perform reconnaissance, escalate privileges, and execute additional payloads. The post TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Asian Government Attacks appear...

item.title: In Other News: ChatGPT Data Leak, Android Rootkit, Water Facility Hit by Ransomware
item.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Symantec vulnerability, anti-ClickFix mechanism added to macOS, FBI hack classified as major incident. The post In Other News: ChatGPT Data Leak, Andro...

item.title: Critical ShareFile Flaws Lead to Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: The vulnerabilities can be chained together to bypass authentication and upload arbitrary files to the server. The post Critical ShareFile Flaws Lead to Unauthenticated RCE appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: CrowdStrike Next-Gen SIEM Can Now Ingest Microsoft Defender Telemetry
item.description: Once CrowdStrike's nemesis, Microsoft is now a collaborator. A shared interest in Formula 1 helped thaw the years-long fierce rivalry.

item.title: Windows Security app gets Secure Boot certificate status indicators as 2026 expiration approaches
item.description: Microsoft’s Secure Boot certificates, issued in 2011, are approaching expiration in 2026. To help IT administrators track whether devices have received replacement certificates, Microsoft has added new status indicato...

item.title: Company that Secretly Records and Publishes Zoom Meetings
item.description: WebinarTV searches the internet for public Zoom invites, joins the meetings, secretly records them, and publishes (alternate link ) the recordings. It doesn’t use the Zoom record feature, so Zoom can’t do anything abo...

item.title: UNC1069 Social Engineering of Axios Maintainer Led to npm Supply Chain Attack
item.description: The maintainer of the Axios npm package has confirmed that the supply chain compromise was the result of a highly-targeted social engineering campaign orchestrated by North Korean threat actors tracked as UNC1069. Mai...

item.title: Why Third-Party Risk Is the Biggest Gap in Your Clients' Security Posture
item.description: The next major breach hitting your clients probably won't come from inside their walls. It'll come through a vendor they trust, a SaaS tool their finance team signed up for, or a subcontractor nobody in IT knows about...

item.title: Mobile Attack Surface Expands as Enterprises Lose Control
item.description: Shadow AI embedded in everyday apps, combined with outdated mobile devices and zero-click exploits, is creating a new and largely unseen mobile risk. The post Mobile Attack Surface Expands as Enterprises Lose Control...

item.title: React2Shell Exploited in Large-Scale Credential Harvesting Campaign
item.description: Using automated scanning and the Nexus Listener collection framework, the hackers compromised over 750 systems. The post React2Shell Exploited in Large-Scale Credential Harvesting Campaign appeared first on SecurityWe...

item.title: Claude Code source leak exploited to spread malware
item.description: A source code leak involving Anthropic’s Claude Code tool quickly escalated into a cybersecurity threat, as attackers seized on the exposed files to lure developers into downloading malware disguised as “unlocked” ver...

item.title: The democratisation of business email compromise fraud
item.description: This week, Martin tells the story of a crime he encountered and how it shows that the threat landscape is changing.

item.title: US Bans All Foreign-Made Consumer Routers
item.description: This is for new routers ; you don’t have to throw away your existing ones: The Executive Branch determination noted that foreign-produced routers (1) introduce “a supply chain vulnerability that could disrupt the U.S....

item.title: RSAC 2026: AI Dominates, But Community Remains Key to Security
item.description: As AI took center stage at this year's conference, experts debated automation, oversight and the evolving role of human intelligence in cybersecurity — despite the US government's notable absence.

item.title: Cisco Patches 9.8 CVSS IMC and SSM Flaws Allowing Remote System Compromise
item.description: Cisco has released updates to address a critical security flaw in the Integrated Management Controller (IMC) that, if successfully exploited, could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass authentication an...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-3502 TrueConf Client Download of Code Without Integrity Check Vulnerabilit...

item.title: Researchers Uncover Mining Operation Using ISO Lures to Spread RATs and Crypto Miners
item.description: A financially motivated operation codenamed REF1695 has been observed leveraging fake installers to deploy remote access trojans (RATs) and cryptocurrency miners since November 2023. "Beyond cryptomining, the threat a...

item.title: The State of Trusted Open Source Report
item.description: In December 2025, we shared the first-ever The State of Trusted Open Source report, featuring insights from our product data and customer base on open source consumption across our catalog of container image projects,...

item.title: Possible US Government iPhone Hacking Tool Leaked
item.description: Wired writes (alternate source ): Security researchers at Google on Tuesday released a report describing what they’re calling “Coruna,” a highly sophisticated iPhone hacking toolkit that includes five complete hacking...

item.title: WhatsApp Alerts 200 Users After Fake iOS App Installed Spyware; Italian Firm Faces Action
item.description: Meta-owned messaging platform WhatsApp said it alerted about 200 users who were tricked into installing a bogus version of its iOS app that was infected with spyware. According to reports from Italian newspaper La Rep...

item.title: How we caught the Axios supply chain attack
item.description: Joe Desimone shares the story of how he caught the Axios supply chain attack with a proof of concept tool built in an afternoon.

item.title: Ransomware Will Hit Hospitals. Rehearsals Are Key to Defense
item.description: A chief medical information officer describes what hospitals face when they inevitably suffer a ransomware attack—whether it leads to short- or long-term outages.

item.title: Threat Brief: Widespread Impact of the Axios Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Unit 42 discusses the supply chain attack targeting Axios. Learn about the full attack chain, from the dropper to forensic cleanup. The post Threat Brief: Widespread Impact of the Axios Supply Chain Attack appeared fi...

item.title: Is “Hackback” Official US Cybersecurity Strategy?
item.description: The 2026 US “ Cyber Strategy for America ” document is mostly the same thing we’ve seen out of the White House for over a decade, but with a more aggressive tone. But one sentence stood out: “We will unleash the priva...

item.title: Cyberattacks Intensify Pressure on Latin American Governments
item.description: Cyber threats across Latin America are increasingly targeting government systems, from disruptive attacks in Puerto Rico to a surge of probes against Colombia’s health sector.

item.title: CERT-UA Impersonation Campaign Spread AGEWHEEZE Malware to 1 Million Emails
item.description: The Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) has disclosed details of a new phishing campaign in which the cybersecurity agency itself was impersonated to distribute a remote administration tool known as...

item.title: Introducing EmDash — the spiritual successor to WordPress that solves plugin security
item.description: Today we are launching the beta of EmDash, a full-stack serverless JavaScript CMS built on Astro 6.0. It combines the features of a traditional CMS with modern security, running plugins in sandboxed Worker isolates.

item.title: 3 Reasons Attackers Are Using Your Trusted Tools Against You (And Why You Don’t See It Coming)
item.description: For years, cybersecurity has followed a familiar model: block malware, stop the attack. Now, attackers are moving on to what’s next. Threat actors now use malware less frequently in favor of what’s already inside your...

item.title: Are We Training AI Too Late?
item.description: Ask the Expert: Cybersecurity teams need to expand their field of view to include new, unique threat sources, rather than relying on past, proven threat actors.

item.title: Claude Code Source Leaked via npm Packaging Error, Anthropic Confirms
item.description: Anthropic on Tuesday confirmed that internal code for its popular artificial intelligence (AI) coding assistant, Claude Code, had been inadvertently released due to a human error. "No sensitive customer data or creden...

item.title: Inside the Axios supply chain compromise - one RAT to rule them all
item.description: Elastic Security Labs analyzes a supply chain compromise of the axios npm package delivering a unified cross-platform RAT

item.title: The Forgotten Endpoint: Security Risks of Dormant Devices
item.description: Technology Talk: That forgotten notebook holds plenty of secrets to enterprise access.

item.title: Supply Chain Attack on Axios Delivers Cross-Platform RAT via Compromised npm Account
item.description: On March 31, 2026, attackers compromised the primary maintainer account of the axios npm package and published two malicious versions that silently installed a cross-platform Remote Access Trojan (RAT) on macOS, Windo...

item.title: AWS Security Agent on-demand penetration testing now generally available
item.description: AWS Security Agent on-demand penetration testing is now generally available, enabling you to run comprehensive security tests across all your applications, not only your most critical ones. This milestone transforms p...

item.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks on Southeast Asian Government Networks
item.description: A high-severity security flaw in the TrueConf client video conferencing software has been exploited in the wild as a zero-day as part of a campaign targeting government entities in Southeast Asia dubbed TrueChaos. The...

item.title: North Korea-Nexus Threat Actor Compromises Widely Used Axios NPM Package in Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Written by: Austin Larsen, Dima Lenz, Adrian Hernandez, Tyler McLellan, Christopher Gardner, Ashley Zaya, Michael Rudden, Mon Liclican Introduction Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) is tracking an active softwar...

item.title: Iran Deploys 'Pseudo-Ransomware,' Revives Pay2Key Operations
item.description: Iranian APTs are blurring the lines between state-sponsored and cybercriminal activities to target high-impact US organizations.

item.title: Introducing Programmable Flow Protection: custom DDoS mitigation logic for Magic Transit customers
item.description: Magic Transit customers can now program their own DDoS mitigation logic and deploy it across Cloudflare’s global network. This enables precise, stateful mitigation for custom and proprietary UDP protocols.

item.title: TeamPCP Explores Ways to Exploit Stolen Supply Chain Secrets
item.description: TeamPCP is exploring ways to monetize the secrets harvested during supply chain attacks, with identified ties to the Lapsus$ and Vect ransomware gangs

item.title: The AI Arms Race – Why Unified Exposure Management Is Becoming a Boardroom Priority
item.description: The cybersecurity landscape is accelerating at an unprecedented rate. What is emerging is not simply a rise in the number of vulnerabilities or tools, but a dramatic increase in speed. Speed of attack, speed of exploi...

item.title: Inventors of Quantum Cryptography Win Turing Award
item.description: Charles Bennett and Gilles Brassard have won the 2026 Turing Award for inventing quantum cryptography. I am incredibly pleased to see them get this recognition. I have always thought the technology to be fantastic, ev...

item.title: Employee Data Breaches Surge to Seven-Year High
item.description: Analysis from law firm Nockolds suggests non-cyber incidents are driving up employee data breaches

item.title: Double Agents: Exposing Security Blind Spots in GCP Vertex AI
item.description: Unit 42 uncovers a "double agent" flaw in Google Cloud's Vertex AI, demonstrating how overprivileged AI agents can compromise cloud environments. The post Double Agents: Exposing Security Blind Spots in GCP Vertex AI...

item.title: Axios Supply Chain Attack Pushes Cross-Platform RAT via Compromised npm Account
item.description: The popular HTTP client known as Axios has suffered a supply chain attack after two newly published versions of the npm package introduced a malicious dependency that delivers a trojan capable of targeting Windows, ma...

item.title: Detecting CVE-2026-20929: Kerberos Authentication Relay via CNAME Abuse
AI-Powered 'DeepLoad' Malware Steals Credentials, Evades Detection
item.description: The massive amount of junk code that hides the malware's logic from security scans was almost certainly generated by AI, researchers say.

item.title: F5 BIG-IP Vulnerability Reclassified as RCE, Under Exploitation
item.description: CVE-2025-53521 was initially disclosed in October as a high-severity denial-of-service (DoS) flaw, but new information has revealed the bug is actually much more dangerous.

item.title: DeepLoad Malware Uses ClickFix and WMI Persistence to Steal Browser Credentials
item.description: A new campaign has leveraged the ClickFix social engineering tactic as a way to distribute a previously undocumented malware loader referred to as DeepLoad. "It likely uses AI-assisted obfuscation and process injectio...

item.title: Storm Brews Over Critical, No-Click Telegram Flaw
item.description: The vulnerability, which is allegedly triggered by a corrupted sticker in the messaging app, received a 9.8 CVSS score, but Telegram denies it exists.

item.title: Cybercriminals Exploit Tax Season With New Phishing Tactics
item.description: Tax-season phishing floods deliver RMM malware, credential theft, BEC and tax-form scams

item.title: DeepLoad Malware Combines ClickFix With AI-Generated Code to Avoid Detection
item.description: Researchers at ReliaQuest warn of persistent malware campaign targeting enterprise credentials

item.title: The State of Secrets Sprawl 2026: 9 Takeaways for CISOs
item.description: Secrets sprawl isn't slowing down: in 2025, it accelerated faster than most security teams anticipated. GitGuardian's State of Secrets Sprawl 2026 report analyzed billions of commits across public GitHub and uncovered...

item.title: Apple’s Camera Indicator Lights
item.description: A thoughtful review of Apple’s system to alert users that the camera is on. It’s really well-designed, and important in a world where malware could surreptitiously start recording. The reason it’s tempting to think th...

item.title: Critical Citrix NetScaler Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
item.description: Researchers from watchTowr and Defused have found evidence that attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2026-3055, a critical NetScaler vulnerability

item.title: ICO Fines UK Nuisance Call Scammers £100,000
item.description: The UK Information Commissioner’s Office has handed a £100,000 fine to Birmingham-based TMAC

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Breach FBI Director’s Personal Email, Hit Stryker With Wiper Attack
item.description: Threat actors with ties to Iran successfully broke into the personal email account of Kash Patel, the director of the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), and leaked a cache of photos and other documents to the...

item.title: Citrix NetScaler Under Active Recon for CVE-2026-3055 (CVSS 9.3) Memory Overread Bug
item.description: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway is witnessing active reconnaissance activity, according to Defused Cyber and watchTowr. The vulnerability, CVE-2026-3055...

item.title: Apple Sends Lock Screen Alerts to Outdated iPhones Over Active Web-Based Exploits
item.description: Apple is now sending Lock Screen notifications to iPhones and iPads running older versions of iOS and iPadOS to alert users of web-based attacks and urge them to install the update. The development was first reported...

item.title: TeamPCP Pushes Malicious Telnyx Versions to PyPI, Hides Stealer in WAV Files
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the supply chain attack targeting Trivy, KICS, and litellm, has now compromised the telnyx Python package by pushing two malicious versions to steal sensitive data. The two versions, 4...

item.title: China Upgrades the Backdoor It Uses to Spy on Telcos Globally
item.description: Chinese APT Red Menshen's super-advanced BPFdoor malware defeats traditional cybersecurity protections. All telcos can do, really, is try hunting it down.

item.title: Wartime Usage of Compromised IP Cameras Highlight Their Danger
item.description: The list of countries exploiting Internet-connected cameras to give them eyes inside their adversaries' borders continues to expand. What should companies look out for?

item.title: New Wave of AiTM Phishing Targets TikTok for Business
item.description: Push Security has uncovered a new AiTM phishing campaign targeting TikTok for Business accounts using Google and TikTok themed login pages

item.title: Quantum Computing Threat to Encryption Is Closer Than Expected, Warns Google
item.description: ‘Q-Day’ and the cybersecurity problems it brings could come as early as 2029 as Google accelerates its post-quantum cryptography migration

item.title: UK Cracks Down on Chinese Crypto Marketplace for Funding Southeast Asia Scam Hubs
item.description: The UK government has sanctioned Xinbi, described as “the second-largest illicit online marketplace ever”

item.title: Unpatchable Vulnerabilities of Kubernetes: CVE-2020-8561
item.description: A look at how Kubernetes CVE-2020-8561 works

item.title: Threat Brief: March 2026 Escalation of Cyber Risk Related to Iran (Updated March 26)
item.description: Unit 42 details recent Iranian cyberattack activity, sharing direct observations of phishing, hacktivist activity and cybercrime. We include recommendations for defenders. The post Threat Brief: March 2026 Escalation...

item.title: Coruna, DarkSword & Democratizing Nation-State Exploit Kits
item.description: Nation-state malware is being sold on the Dark Web and leaked to GitHub; and ordinary organizations might not stand much of a chance of defending themselves.

item.title: Automotive Cybersecurity Threats Grow in Era of Connected, Autonomous Vehicles
item.description: More than a decade since the 2015 Jeep hack, the cybersecurity of vehicles remains of the utmost importance.

item.title: Critical Flaw in Langflow AI Platform Under Attack
item.description: Threats actors pounced on the code injection vulnerability within hours of its disclosure, demonstrating that organizations have little time to address critical bugs.

item.title: Attackers Rapidly Weaponize Critical Oracle WebLogic RCE, Honeypot Study Finds
item.description: Attackers rapidly exploited a critical Oracle WebLogic RCE flaw the same day exploit code was released, according to a CloudSEK honeypot study

item.title: How Organizations Can Use Mistakes to Level Up Their Security Programs
item.description: Organizations repeatedly expose ports, reuse passwords, and skip patches, creating security gaps that attackers exploit for breaches. An industry veteran outlines ways to fix these common mistakes.

item.title: EtherRAT Techniques Bypass Security Via Ethereum Smart Contracts
item.description: EtherRAT hides C2 in Ethereum smart contracts via EtherHiding, steals wallets and credentials

item.title: AI-Powered Dependency Decisions Introduce, Ignore Security Bugs
item.description: AI models often hallucinate or make costly mistakes when tasked with recommending software versions, upgrade paths, and security fixes — leading to significant technical debt.

item.title: AI Becomes the Top Cybersecurity Priority for Defenders as Criminals Exploit It, PwC Warns
item.description: PwC Annual Threat Dynamics report says AI-threats are the biggest concern of clients

item.title: A one-line Kubernetes fix that saved 600 hours a year
item.description: When we investigated why our Atlantis instance took 30 minutes to restart, we discovered a bottleneck in how Kubernetes handles volume permissions. By adjusting the fsGroupChangePolicy, we reduced restart times to 30...

item.title: Introducing the Green Agent: AI-Powered Remediation for the Cloud
item.description: Accelerate your path to Zero Criticals with AI that investigates, assigns, and guides cloud remediation for you

item.title: Talos Takes: 2025 insights from Talos and Splunk
item.description: This episode of Talos Takes breaks down the 2025 Year in Review as well as Splunk's Top 50 Cybersecurity Threats report.

item.title: OpenAI Expands Bug Bounty to Cover AI Abuse and 'Safety' Concerns
item.description: OpenAI’s Safety Bug Bounty program seeks to address AI safety vulnerabilities beyond traditional security flaws

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-33634 Aqua Security Trivy Embedded Malicious Code Vulnerability This type...

item.title: Iran-Linked Pay2Key Ransomware Group Re-Emerges
item.description: Halcyon and Beazley Security track the return of Iranian ransomware group Pay2Key

item.title: Invoice Fraud Costs UK Construction Sector Millions, NCA Warns
item.description: The National Crime Agency has warned construction firms about surging invoice fraud

item.title: AI infrastructure security: Why it deserves its own category
item.description: Attacks on artificial intelligence infrastructure are rising, but not in the way most people expect. While AI security headlines focus on prompt manipulation, attackers are going after the infrastructure behind these...

item.title: Three pillars for building effective runtime-powered cloud defense, the right way
item.description: To secure your cloud, you have to secure it at runtime. Here’s how to build a runtime-powered cloud defense that secures your infrastructure in real time.

item.title: Closing the cloud security gap with runtime security
item.description: Imagine your cloud environment as a commercial plane. Before flight, planes undergo strict maintenance schedules, tests, and preflight checks to…

item.title: Illuminating VoidLink: Technical analysis of the VoidLink rootkit framework
item.description: Elastic Security Labs analyzes VoidLink, a sophisticated Linux malware framework that combines traditional Loadable Kernel Modules with eBPF to maintain persistence.

item.title: At RSAC, the EU Leads While US Officials Are Sidelined
item.description: While US government sits out this year, EU officials are on the ground in San Francisco leading the conversations on today's top cybersecurity challenges.

item.title: Introducing Wiz Workflows: Your path to building a self healing cloud
item.description: Orchestrate customizable workflows with agents, enabling end-to-end discovery and response in Wiz

item.title: Cloud Phones Linked to Rising Financial Fraud Threat
item.description: Cloud Android phones fuel financial fraud, evading detection and enabling dropper accounts

item.title: Hackers Exploit Compromised Enterprise Identities at Industrial Scale, Warns SentinelOne
item.description: Cybersecurity company’s annual report issues warning over a “mass-marketed impersonation crisis” over attackers abusing legitimate credentials

item.title: Phishers Pose as Palo Alto Networks' Recruiters for Months in Job Scam
item.description: A series of campaigns that began in August aim to defraud job candidates, using psychological tactics and data scraped from LinkedIn profiles.

item.title: AI Dominates RSAC Innovation Sandbox
item.description: Ten finalists had three minutes to make their case for being the most innovative, promising young security company of the year. Geordie AI wins the 2026 contest.
